meta.architecture: Thin client SvelteKit frontend consuming SDK that points to cloud Rust/Actix server
meta.description: Activity/experience business admin and customer portal
meta.navigation_philosophy: zoom in/zoom out with canonical routes and ?from= param for back-nav context

roles.inheritance: each role inherits all permissions of roles below it

descriptions.admin: Full access. Refunds, user management, settings, destructive actions.
descriptions.editor: CRUD on bookings, customers, orders. View all data. No refunds or user management. Inherits operator.
descriptions.operator: Front-of-house. View daily bookings, advance service states, manage queue, mark photos taken. Inherits client.
descriptions.client: Customer portal. View/submit own bookings, orders, waivers, surveys. View public photo albums.

roles.notes: Role-to-business-title mapping (admin=exec/owner, editor=manager, operator=wait staff, client=customer) is managed in app config, not hardcoded.

two_track_model.description: Two parallel tracks converge at the service fulfillment layer. Visually separated in UI.

track_1_reservations.description: Standard online bookings made before configurable cutoff. Confirmed time slots. Paid online. Has order record.

track_2_queue.description: Priority waiting list for guests outside booking window. No order record. POS payment tracked via product_id + headcount at activation.
track_2_queue.data_flow: queue_entry -> arrived -> active (product_id + headcount assigned, check_in_group created) -> complete

online_call_ahead.description: Booked online after cutoff. Higher priority in queue.

walk_up.description: No prior notification. Added by operator on arrival.

track_2_queue.ordering: Priority tier first, then timestamp within tier

two_track_model.convergence_point: Both tracks create a check_in_group once guest is active. See command-center.json for the 8-state service machine on check_in_group.

cutoff_window.description: Hours before event. Before cutoff = Track 1. After cutoff = Track 2 (online call-ahead tier).

reschedule_policy.effect: Resets booking to waiting on new date regardless of prior state
reschedule_policy.second_miss: Booking flagged as gift_certificate with credit_amount = order value

day_close.trigger: Shift-open hook. First authenticated login (operator+) of the next business day.
day_close.mechanism: On login, check last_closed_date in settings. If stale, run close routine for all unclosed days, update last_closed_date.
day_close.grace_condition: Do not auto-complete entries where check_in_group service_state is currently playing. These require manual resolution by editor+.
day_close.behavior: All non-complete booking and queue entries for closed day(s) marked complete.

capture_tracking.description: Per-booking/queue-entry boolean flag on check_in_group record. Photographer marks photos_taken during the day. Operational indicator so photographer can see which groups still need shooting.
capture_tracking.purpose: Day-of operational tracking. Not tied to album system.

album_management.description: Post-day curation and delivery. Decoupled from capture tracking.

album.creation: Auto-created by CLI on upload. Staff can rename.
album.visibility: public | private. Set by CLI flag on creation. Staff can toggle.

photos.upload: Bulk via CLI into date album
photos.management: Admin UI: add missing photos, remove individual photos
photos.ordering: Default by upload time. Optional sort_position field for manual reorder (nice-to-have, not critical).

album_management.delivery: Public albums served via unauthenticated API endpoint for the website. Publishing album = delivery complete.

/command-center.description: Split view of the two primary ops lists, bookings on the left and queue on the right. Primary way staff engages with state machine.

/bookings/[id].description: Single booking detail — name, phone, email, address, package, headcount, service status, photos_taken flag, reschedule count, gift cert flag

/queue/[id].description: Queue entry detail — name, contact, party size, tier, product (when active), headcount (when active), service status, photos_taken flag

use_cases: Manager arrives and logs in, looks at the bookings for the day. Notes that the first booking is an hour after open and no guests in queue. Moves on to other tasks.

Manager arrives and logs in, notices there are 2 paid bookings at open. Directs staff to begin prep. Staff updates the state-machine with `prepped` and `filled` states. The groups check in on time with the exact number of guests. The manager marks the booking as `arrived`, checks waivers, and marks group as `checked-in`. Group goes to safety orientation with staff. Staff updates the state machine setting the `safety-orientation` flag. One group requires photos. When done, the photographer updates the state machine with `photos` done.
Some groups show up with extra players, headcounts get updates for prep team, waivers are collected and payment is taken on the POS. Proceeds as normal.

Some groups show up in bunches. The first bunch may want to start without waiting for the remaining members of the party. Manager activates a split-checkin feature which converts one digital booking into two visual bookings (active and waiting). Manager checks waivers, issues wristbands and the group can start. Late players are removed from the waiting split and converted to active. When all players arrive the split is removed and resolved visually.

Some groups show up short players. In this case, guests get what they paid for, the split system is activated but flagged as fulfilled. Inevitably players will arrive. The only thing the cashiers need to do is collect a waiver and issue a wristband.

/bookings.description: Day-view list of standard online bookings (Track 1) with date nav. Visually separated from queue.

/bookings/[id].description: Single booking detail — name, phone, email, address, package, headcount, service status, photos_taken flag, reschedule count, gift cert flag

/queue.description: Day-view priority queue (Track 2). Public visibility per DEC-070. Online call-aheads above walk-ups.

/queue/[id].description: Queue entry detail — name, contact, party size, tier, product (when active), headcount (when active), service status, photos_taken flag

/customers/[id].description: Customer detail — order history, LTV, survey responses

/orders.description: Order list with filters. Track 1 only.

/orders/[id].description: Order detail — amount, IP address, auth responses, refund workflow

/waivers.description: Waiver list by date or booking/queue entry

/albums.description: Album list by date. Shows name, date, visibility, photo count.

/albums/[id].description: Album detail — photo grid/thumbnails. Rename, toggle visibility, add/remove photos.

/settings.description: App settings, user management, role assignments, cutoff config

/portal/queue.description: Client views own queue position (call-ahead only)

POST /login.description: Authenticate user. Triggers shift-open day-close check for operator+ roles.

GET /session.description: Current session with role and permissions

POST /[id]/reschedule.description: Reschedule to new date. Resets to waiting. Enforces 1-reschedule limit.

GET /.description: Day queue ordered by priority then timestamp. Public per DEC-070.

GET /[id].description: Queue entry detail. Public per DEC-070.

POST /[id]/activate.description: Activate entry. Assigns product + headcount. Creates default check_in_group.

POST /[id]/unskip.description: Return skipped entry to active (before day close)

POST /[group_id]/photos-taken.description: Toggle photos_taken flag on check-in group

POST /[parent_id]/split.description: Split a check-in group. See command-center.json split_mechanics.

POST /.description: Upload photo(s) to album (also used by CLI)

GET /.description: List public albums. No auth required. For website consumption.

GET /[id].description: Public album detail with photo URLs. No auth required.

POST /check.description: Shift-open hook. Checks last_closed_date. If stale, closes unclosed days. Grace: skips playing entries.

skipped.action: Reschedule to new date, reset state, increment reschedule_count

booking_lifecycle.auto_complete: Day-close marks non-complete entries as complete (grace: skips entries with check_in_group in playing state)

business_rules.reschedule: Resets to waiting on new date. Allowed once.
business_rules.second_miss: reschedule_count >= 1 and skipped again -> editor converts to gift_certificate

queue_lifecycle.auto_complete: Day-close marks all non-complete entries as complete (grace: skips entries with check_in_group in playing state)

state_machines._service_state_machine: Defined in command-center.json — 8-state machine on check_in_group entity.

order_lifecycle.notes: Track 1 only. in_cart -> failed is system-driven. Refund is admin-only.

products.description: Product/package catalog. Pricing source of truth.

albums.description: Date-based photo albums. Created by CLI, curated by staff.

fields.name: string (default: date string, staff can rename)
fields.visibility: public | private (set by CLI flag, toggleable by staff)

navigation.back_nav: ?from= query param for contextual back button
